title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
summary: An AI news video covering multiple recent developments including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, Claude Fable discontinuation, MiniMax M3, and various new tools for video generation, avatars, and translation. The host highlights open-source releases and new model capabilities across text, video, and multimodal domains.
writeup: This AI Search roundup surveys recent releases across several model categories: new language models (GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3), video generation systems (VideoMDM, Millivid), text-to-speech (Dots TTS), translation via Gemini Live Translate, and 3D body and avatar generation through Flex4DHuman. It also notes the discontinuation of Claude Fable and surveys emerging agent frameworks and research models. The breadth matters because it tracks simultaneous progress across open-source and proprietary systems in language, video, speech, and avatars. Recurring technical threads include sparse attention and AI agents.
